items.summary: Trump warned that the U.S. will target Iranian infrastructure next week if negotiations do not resume.

items.summary: The technological rivalry between China and the U.S. has intensified, as they race for AI dominance.

items.title: Alpine Texworld IPO sails through on Day 3: Check subscription status, GMP and other key details
items.summary: Alpine Texworld's IPO was fully subscribed on the final day, achieving 1.40 times overall subscription. The company aims to raise ₹126 crore with fresh shares to fund a new weaving unit in Ahmedabad. Allotments are scheduled for July 17, with listing on BSE and NSE expected on July 21.

items.title: NHIT appoints general management consultants to improve National Highway operations and maintenance
items.summary: To improve the management of highway operations, National Highways Infra Trust has partnered with consultants. This initiative focuses on maximizing asset performance by implementing international best practices. The consultants will carry out thorough inspections to identify maintenance necessities for users and will oversee contractor selection and task supervision. By adopting this model, the Trust looks to bolster investor trust and promote increased investments in highway assets.

items.title: Ex-Fed advisor gets over three years in prison for lying about China ties
items.summary: The case marks one of the most prominent U.S. prosecutions alleging Chinese intelligence targeting of U.S. institutions, as Trump intensifies its pursuit of foreign economic espionage.

items.title: Wall Street slips as retail sales growth cools, AI stock valuation concerns weigh on markets
items.summary: In the early trading, the Dow Jones Industrial Average was up 0.2% at 52,751.45. The broad-based S&P 500 declined 0.4% to 7,543.96, while the tech-rich Nasdaq Composite Index dropped 0.8% to 26,051.43

items.title: Investigation into parking tickets for drivers queuing at petrol stations
items.summary: It's part of a wider crackdown from the consumer regulator into potentially unfair practices by private parking operators.

items.title: India to come out soon with export-oriented fisheries policy with focus on inland fishery
items.summary: Animal Husbandry and Fisheries Minister Rajiv Ranjan Sing asks ICAR to explore using drones for transporting the products and inputs for farming could be a solution

items.title: RBI bars banks, NBFCs from selling acquired stressed assets back to defaulting borrowers, related parties
items.summary: New Reserve Bank of India norms will prevent lenders from reselling acquired assets to defaulting borrowers. These prudential rules apply to banks, small finance banks, and NBFCs starting October 2026. Specified non-financial assets are those obtained when resolving stressed loans from borrowers. Lenders must establish board-approved policies for acquiring and disposing of these assets. These assets will be disclosed separately on balance sheets, not as NPAs.

items.title: RBI's financial inclusion index rises 4.48 pc in FY26
items.summary: The Reserve Bank's Financial Inclusion Index increased by 4.48 percent. This index measures financial inclusion across India's banking and insurance sectors. The value for March 2026 reached 70.0, up from 67.0 in March 2025. Growth was observed across all sub-indices, reflecting deepening financial inclusion. Usage of financial services significantly contributed to this overall improvement.

items.title: NISM proposes common test for SIF, MF distributors
items.summary: NISM-Series-V-D certification examination will be available from July 22 and has been designated as the requisite certification standard for individuals involved in the sale and distribution of mutual funds and SIFs

items.title: Treasury yields rise, as oil resumes climb on mounting U.S.-Iran tensions
items.summary: Oil prices resumed their climb on Thursday, as traders monitored mounting tensions between the United States and Iran.

items.title: SBI Funds Management gets highest applications for any IPO this year, sees Rs 2.98 lakh crore investor rush
items.summary: SBI Funds Management's Rs 9,813 crore IPO saw 42 times subscription on its final day. Qualified institutional buyers led the strong demand, subscribing 140.11 times their allocated portion. The issue received nearly 65 lakh applications, the highest number for any IPO this year. The company, India's largest asset manager, is expected to list with a double-digit premium.

items.title: US stocks today: S&P 500, Nasdaq open lower as chip stocks weaken
items.summary: U.S. markets opened mixed with Dow gaining while S&P 500 and Nasdaq slipped as chip stocks continued to weaken. Investors assessed fresh economic data for signals on growth and policy direction. The divergence highlights sector rotation and cautious sentiment amid uncertainty around tech performance and broader economic conditions.

items.title: Eli Lilly to buy psychedelics maker AtaiBeckley for $2.8 billion as experimental treatments gain traction
items.summary: The deal for AtaiBeckley gives Lilly access to experimental drugs based on DMT and MDMA.

items.summary: Addressing a conference organised by industry body FICCI, he cited a recent report by Crisil which highlighted that the food processing level has increased to 17 per cent in 2023 from 10 per cent in 2010-11.

items.title: Jio Financial Services Q1 results: Net profit jumps 156% YoY to ₹830 crore, revenue tops ₹2,000 crore
items.summary: Jio Financial Services reported a Q1 FY27 net profit of ₹830 crore, up 156% YoY and 205% sequentially. Revenue soared to ₹2,004 crore, reflecting 223% growth. Despite rising expenses, strong core income drove profit growth, with total income increasing 141% YoY to ₹1,496 crore.

items.title: Jio Financial Q1 Results: Profit skyrockets 155% YoY to Rs 830 crore
items.summary: Jio Financial on Thursday reported 155% year-on-year (YoY) growth in its consolidated net profit at Rs 830 crore in the first quarter. The same stood at Rs 325 crore in the same quarter last year.

items.title: Indian rice export prices extend gain on higher reserve price, monsoon concerns
items.summary: Indian rice export prices have increased for a third consecutive week. This rise is due to higher reserve prices for government sales. Floods in Bangladesh have damaged crops on over twenty-eight thousand hectares. Thai traders remain cautious due to potential El Nino impacts on production. Vietnamese rice prices are unchanged as buyers await increased harvest supplies.

items.title: Nvidia-backed Fireworks hits $17.5 billion valuation as companies pursue cheaper AI models
items.summary: Fireworks once relied heavily on revenue from coding startup Cursor, but has diversified in the past year as more companies reach for lower-cost AI models.

items.title: Nvidia unveils new AI model and expands Japan’s physical AI ecosystem
items.summary: Nvidia announces new AI model, Cosmos 3 Edge, and expansion of its physical AI ecosystems in Japan.

items.title: HDFC Mutual Fund buys 9.3 lakh shares of Jupiter Life Hospitals for ₹139 cr
items.summary: Following the transaction, shares of Jupiter Life Line Hospitals on Thursday rose 2.53 per cent to trade at ₹1,537.60 apiece on the exchange

items.title: Uber in $15 billion deal for Delivery Hero to create global takeout giant
items.summary: The acquisition advances the U.S. ride-hailing firm's efforts to build a global food-delivery business that can better compete with Just Eat, owned by Dutch group Prosus, and U.S. rival DoorDash

items.title: Domestic tourism powering travel boom, but foreign visitor spending flat, says apex tourism body
items.summary: Domestic tourism in India is thriving, with spending rising significantly since 2019, while international visitor expenditure remains flat. The World Travel & Tourism Council calls for stronger collaboration between the government and the private sector to boost inbound tourism.

items.title: US Data Watch: 13 Nasdaq 100 stocks deliver multibaggers return in CY26
items.summary: Thirteen Nasdaq 100 stocks have turned multibaggers in CY26, led by AI, semiconductors and data infrastructure themes, significantly outperforming the broader index and delivering exceptional gains to investors.

items.title: BHEL among 6 midcap stocks that hit 52-week highs and rallied up to 20% in a month
items.summary: Despite a flat Sensex close, six BSE 150 MidCap stocks—Ipca Laboratories, Sona BLW Precision Forgings, BHEL, Gujarat Fluorochemicals, Lupin and Biocon—hit fresh 52-week highs, signalling strong buying interest, improving investor confidence and sustained bullish momentum.

items.title: Polycab Q1 Results: Profit surges 33% YoY to Rs 797 crore, revenue jumps 39%
items.summary: Polycab India delivered its strongest-ever first quarter, with revenue rising 39% and net profit increasing 33%, driven by robust wires and cables demand and strong FMEG growth. Management expects infrastructure spending, capacity expansion and distribution growth to sustain long-term momentum.

items.title: Development of India's highest commercial Lilium cultivation field starts in Ladakh
items.summary: India's highest commercial Lilium cultivation field development has commenced in Ladakh. Over fifty thousand premium Lilium bulbs were planted, with the first bloom expected soon. This high-altitude floriculture project aims to create sustainable livelihoods for local communities. The agriculture department will manage the field initially, then transfer it to self-help groups. Marketing support will be provided to help access national and international markets.

items.title: RMZ to sell ‘office condos’ for ₹6,500 crore to family offices
items.summary: Office leasing and investment volumes in commercial real estate have been rising in India. The growth in investment volume comes on the back of sustained interest from domestic institutions, family offices, and global capital markets players

items.title: India must undertake visa reforms, invest more in tourism: WTTC president
items.summary: India's ability to attract more foreign tourists hinges on critical visa reforms and improved connectivity. The World Travel and Tourism Council underscores the opportunity for fifty million new jobs in the sector. Presently, it is domestic demand that propels growth, though the travel and tourism industry's impact on the GDP in 2025 is undeniably significant. Enhanced marketing investments are vital for boosting international visibility.

items.title: US stock market today: Wall Street futures edge lower as chip stocks retreat; crude oil extends gains
items.summary: US stock futures fluctuated as investors remained cautious over Middle East tensions. While S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures fell, Dow futures gained. Investors are looking toward tech earnings, and oil prices rose after renewed conflicts over Iran threaten global energy supply routes.

items.title: Which used car valuation tool is most accurate? A 2026 India buyer guide
items.summary: How online used car valuation tools work in India, why the estimate changes after inspection, and how to read these numbers as a buyer.

items.title: Market wrap: ICICI Lombard, IndiGo among top gainers & losers on Nifty and Sensex today
items.summary: Indian equities ended subdued on weekly expiry as investors weighed Iran-US tensions, firm crude prices and rupee weakness. Analysts expect Q1 earnings to drive stock-specific action, while Nifty remains in a consolidation phase with key support near 24,000 and resistance around 24,300.

items.title: Perps are distracting Wall Street, not disrupting it
items.summary: Craze for perpetual futures is worrying investors in securities exchanges

items.title: India's energy storage capacity expected to rise more than tenfold by FY2035-36: MoSPI
items.summary: India's energy storage requirement is projected to increase more than tenfold over the next decade, rising from 87 GWh in 2027-28 to 888 GWh by 2035-36, driven by rapid deployment of Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) and Pumped Storage Plants (PSPs), according to the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation's (MoSPI) latest quarterly update of its infrastructure Performance Monitoring Dashboard.

items.title: JSW MG Motor India to invest ₹1,400 crore in Halol plant, launch four new vehicles this fiscal
items.summary: JSW MG Motor India is investing Rs 1400 crore to increase production capacity significantly. The company plans to introduce four new vehicles into the mainstream market this year. Production capacity will rise to 160,000 units annually by fiscal year-end. A battery electric and plug-in hybrid vehicle will launch shortly. The company aims for 70-80% of total sales from new energy vehicles.

items.title: Dow Jones| Nasdaq | S&P 500 | US Stock Market Today | Live: S&P 500, Nasdaq open lower as chip stocks weaken
United earnings top estimates but airline expects $6 billion in added fuel costs
items.summary: United reported higher revenue for premium, corporate and no-frills basic economy tickets and higher revenue for both domestic and international trips.

items.title: U.S. slaps 25% tariff on most Brazilian goods over 'unfair trade practices'
items.summary: A separate U.S. probe into forced-labor enforcement could see an additional 12.5% duty on Brazilian goods on top of the 25%, with the decision due next week.

items.title: Students get nearly 10,000 more MBBS seats this year
items.summary: India will offer a record 1,36,939 MBBS seats for the 2026-27 academic session. The National Medical Commission approved 9,911 additional undergraduate seats, expanding capacity significantly. This expansion comes after revised regulations eased norms for seat increases at colleges. Nearly seventy-nine percent of new seats are in private medical colleges. The increased intake aims to strengthen India's healthcare workforce.

items.title: Petrol, diesel sales surge in first half of July
items.summary: Petrol and diesel sales saw significant year-on-year increases in early July. Below-normal monsoon rains boosted demand from farmers and motorists across India. Diesel sales, a key economic indicator, rose over twenty percent compared to last year. Petrol sales also climbed substantially, though they fell from June's levels. Jet fuel sales inched up, while LPG consumption continued its decline.

items.title: Refurbished electronics go mainstream as organised retailers expand beyond value buyers
items.summary: India's refurbished electronics market is growing rapidly with organized players. Stronger quality standards and warranties are attracting premium buyers now. This shift is moving demand from smaller cities to metro areas. Supply chain infrastructure is becoming crucial for scaling recommerce businesses. Companies are expanding physical retail to build consumer trust and confidence.

items.title: India’s oilmeal exports decline 21.5% in April
items.summary: The decline is attributed to Red Sea shipping disruptions, higher freight costs, and intense price competition from South American soybean meal.

items.title: Indian bonds rise on steady oil, easing US rate-hike fears
items.summary: On Thursday, Indian government bonds experienced an upturn as oil prices stabilized alongside declining U.S. rate hike expectations, enhancing investor confidence. The market eagerly anticipates updates on India's possible inclusion in the Bloomberg index this month, a move that could significantly boost foreign investments in Indian debt markets. Bond yields have decreased for the second consecutive session, mirroring the trends of U.S. Treasury yields.

items.title: BHEL shares jump 4% after Maharatna PSU posts net profit of Rs 377 crore in Q1, revenue jumps 40%
items.summary: BHEL reported a consolidated Q1 FY27 net profit of ₹377 crore against a year-ago loss, driven by 40% revenue growth and a sharp recovery in operating margins. Strong power segment performance lifted sentiment, sending the PSU stock over 4% higher.

items.title: Flat finish masks turmoil; Nifty holds ground as crude, rupee weigh
items.summary: Nifty 50 closed at 24,072.75, down 0.02%; Sensex ended at 77,186.87, up 1.40 points

items.title: BHEL reports Rs 376.71 crore profit for Q1FY27
items.summary: Bharat Heavy Electricals Ltd posted a profit of Rs 376.71 crore for the June quarter. This marks a substantial recovery from the Rs 455.50 crore net loss reported last year. Total income increased to Rs 7,911.86 crore, up from Rs 5,658.07 crore previously. The power segment drove this turnaround, showing strong revenue and profit growth. Industry segment revenue also rose, though its profit saw a slight decrease.

items.title: ITC sees demand momentum sustaining in H2; bets on premiumisation and healthy products
items.summary: ITC anticipates strong demand in the latter half of FY26, boosted by festive celebrations. Premium and super-premium product growth is evident in both urban and rural markets. Consumers now prioritize quality and understand product benefits beyond just price. Elevated edible oil prices and weather uncertainties remain key concerns for the company. ITC is expanding its health-oriented product portfolio to meet evolving consumer preferences.

items.title: India's food processing sector enters value-led growth phase, offers USD 600 bn opportunity by 2030: Report
items.summary: India's food processing sector is shifting towards value-led growth by 2030. This transformation will drive the industry to a USD 600 billion valuation. Innovation and technology adoption will fuel this significant expansion. Consumers increasingly demand healthier and convenient premium food products. The government aims to boost exports and create new employment opportunities.

items.title: Cellecor Gadgets aims to achieve ₹5,000-crore revenue in three years: Ravi Agarwal
items.summary: Cellecor Gadgets is aiming for ₹5,000 crore revenue within three years. Consumer appliances and smart TVs offer significant long-term growth opportunities. The company is investing in a manufacturing unit in Liberia, West Africa. Its offline distribution network contributes over ninety per cent of total revenue.

items.title: Exim Bank India presents Rs 428 cr dividend cheque to FM Sitharaman for FY26
items.summary: EXIM Bank of India presented a Rs 428 crore dividend cheque to Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman. The bank reported a net profit of Rs 4,273 crore for the financial year 2025-26. Ten percent of this net profit was transferred as a balance to the government of India. Exim Bank recorded thirty-two percent growth in net profit and robust financial indicators.

items.title: From handcart in 1960 to 1,300 flavours: How an Ahmedabad family built an Ice cream brand
items.summary: What began as a hand-churned ice cream cart on the streets of Ahmedabad in 1960 has grown into a premium ice cream brand with more than 1,300 proprietary flavours. The journey of Shankar Ice Cream Library spans over six decades of adapting to changing customer preferences.

items.title: Blackstone appoints Ami Momaya to lead India infrastructure investments
items.summary: Momaya joins as managing director and head of infrastructure for India, as the private equity firm builds its first dedicated infrastructure platform in Asia Pacific.

items.title: British Steel taken into public ownership to protect 'vital' UK supply
items.summary: The Scunthorpe steelworks has been officially nationalised under new government powers passed this week.

items.title: India, UK trade pact to enhance exports competitiveness, slash trade costs: Experts
items.summary: The India-UK free trade pact is expected to reduce trade costs significantly. Domestic goods will gain enhanced competitiveness in the British market. Labour-intensive sectors now enter the UK market with zero duty. Experts believe this agreement will achieve a USD 100 billion bilateral trade target. Indian enterprises must now leverage these commitments for tangible outcomes.

items.title: Quote of the day by Joel Greenblatt: "When it comes to long-term investing, doing “less” is often “more”
items.summary: Value investor Joel Greenblatt advises that less action yields more long-term investment gains. Patience and disciplined holding outperform frequent trading and market timing. Compounding wealth requires staying invested in strong businesses through market ups and downs. Avoiding emotional reactions to price swings is crucial for investment success. Resisting the urge to act and letting investments grow proves most rewarding.

items.title: ITC Hotels reports net profit of Rs 182 cr for Q1FY27, revenue at Rs 936 cr
items.summary: ITC Hotels achieved Rs 936 crore revenue and Rs 182 crore profit in Q1 FY27. The company also acquired Welcomhotel Ahmedabad for Rs 155 crore. Kumarakom Resort & Spa acquisition is complete, opening as a luxury resort. Eight new properties were signed, and the managed portfolio now exceeds 200 hotels. The hospitality sector outlook remains positive due to India's economic growth.

items.title: Aer Lingus proposes cutting 500 jobs under savings plan
items.summary: The company proposes cutting 290 roles in its head office, along with 140 cabin roles and 70 pilots.

items.title: Delhi Development Authority earns ₹1,020 crore from housing sales in Q1 FY27
items.summary: Sub city Narela emerged as the biggest contributor to this success, with 1,153 flats sold during the quarter, accounting for nearly 90% of the total sales, which shows the increasing attractiveness of Narela as a preferred residential destination. This growth is being supported by DDA's continuous infrastructure development efforts, including key projects such as the Urban Extension Road-II (UER-II) and the upcoming Rithala-Narela-Kundli Metro corridor, which are significantly improving connectivity.

items.title: Piramal Finance posts 67% jump in Q1 profit, board clears ₹4,000 crore fundraise
items.summary: The stock closed at ₹2,186.50 on the NSE on Thursday, up 0.76% on the day, giving the company a market capitalisation of approximately ₹49,620 crore.

items.title: Wipro declares interim dividend worth Rs 2/share, fixes record date. Check details
items.summary: Wipro declared an interim dividend of ₹2 per share, with July 27 fixed as the record date. The IT major reported modest Q1 FY27 profit and revenue growth, issued cautious quarterly guidance and reiterated its focus on AI-led business transformation.

items.title: AI-driven cyberthreats spark revenue, funding surge for cybersecurity startups
items.summary: Investor interest in cybersecurity has strengthened with funding that almost doubled to $89 million in H1 2026 from $47 million in H1 2025 earlier, according to Venture Intelligence.

items.title: SBI Mutual Fund CEO Mishra outlines four priorities to drive growth
items.summary: SBI MF's Mishra has outlined four priorities as the fund house aims to double AUM and its customer base in five years. The push comes as parent SBI's asset management arm wraps up a strongly subscribed IPO, with its Amundi partnership unchanged.

items.title: Hero MotoCorp enters Germany with KSR Group distribution partnership
items.summary: Hero MotoCorp has officially entered the German market through a new distribution partnership. This expansion marks a significant milestone for the company in Europe's largest economy. The company will introduce its Euro 5+ compliant products, led by the XPulse 200 4V series. KSR Group will manage sales and service through over twenty-eight outlets across major German cities. This move makes Germany Hero MotoCorp's fifth European and fifty-third global market.

items.title: Oil market absorbs war shock through buffers, but stocks run thin: IMF
items.summary: Crude prices settled into a relatively stable range of $90-100 per barrel despite the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Dixon Tech, Kaynes, Cyient DLM jump after Cabinet clears ₹62,500 crore for mobile PLI 2.0
items.summary: Syrma SGS Technology and Amber Enterprises also trade higher

items.title: Jana SFB shares rise 2% as Q1 profit jumps 52%
items.summary: The rally came after the bank reported a net profit of ₹155 crore for the quarter ended June 2026, up 52% from ₹102 crore in the corresponding quarter of the previous financial year.

items.title: Price hikes push buyers towards refurbished smartphones and laptops
items.summary: India's refurbished device market will significantly outperform new sales this year. Rising memory costs are pushing up prices for new smartphones and laptops. Certified pre-owned products are becoming a more attractive option for consumers. The refurbished smartphone market is projected to grow twelve percent in 2026.

items.title: TikTok faces Ofcom investigation over child age checks
items.summary: It follows a review by the regulator in May that criticised the platform for not being "safe enough" for children.

items.title: Larry Page, Sergey Brin get $15 billion richer as Warren Buffett says Alphabet bet was his call
items.summary: Warren Buffett revealed he personally initiated Berkshire Hathaway’s investment in Alphabet, lifting the stock nearly 4% and adding over $15 billion to the combined wealth of Google co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin. Berkshire now holds Alphabet among its top five investments, while strong AI-driven growth, cloud expansion, and increased spending plans continue to support investor confidence.

items.title: Prestige Estates Q1 sales bookings down 46 pc to Rs 6,579 cr
items.summary: Prestige Estates Projects Ltd reported a 46 percent sales booking decline for Q1 FY27. This decrease occurred due to a high base effect from the prior year. Hyderabad led quarterly sales, contributing 49 percent of the total revenue. The company completed three projects during the June quarter, adding significant developable area. Prestige Estates aims for Rs 35,000-36,000 crore in sales bookings this fiscal year.

items.title: PepsiCo's India confidence remains intact despite H2 uncertainties, CEO says
items.summary: PepsiCo India anticipates a cautiously optimistic performance for the second half of 2026. Consumer demand remains resilient across urban and rural markets despite economic challenges. The company continues to monitor external uncertainties like weather disruptions and global issues. PepsiCo is investing significantly in India, which is a top global priority market. New manufacturing facilities in Assam and Ujjain highlight this strong commitment to local production.

items.title: Softer inflation trips rate hike forecasts, RBI hold more likely

ET’s interactions with bank economists and a review of research reports show that most institutions now expect the RBI to remain on hold through FY27, while some believe any rate hikes would be back-ended later in the year as the central bank prioritises growth over inflation management.

The sowing season for these crops will remain open until August 15.

It also deepens cooperation in technology, services, and skilled talent mobility.
